description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>workflows/phases/04-build/README.md — Build execution is driven by helix build or helix run, following the bounded loop defined in .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/actions/implementation.md. Use the phase artifacts under .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/phases/04-build/artifacts/ when you need supporting build documentation or issue guidance.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/04-build/README.md:53
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit cc30a3c9b49e68125d0960369a7f9207002e6068 updates the iterate gate to the built-in bead store, but the shipped build-phase workflow bundle still tells operators that story-level work is tracked in .ddx/issues.jsonl. workflows/phases/04-build/README.md:53 and workflows/phases/04-build/artifacts/implementation-plan/template.md:29 now contradict the iterate gate, scripts/helix prompts, and the repo-wide ddx bead contract, so a freshly generated build plan still points users at the legacy tracker path.
Suggested fix: Update the build-phase README and implementation-plan template to describe the built-in bead tracker at .ddx/beads.jsonl, or refer generically to the ddx bead tracker surface, so workflow docs and templates stay consistent with the current tracker contract.

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh — expected_command='ddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding,area:testing' stale_command='ddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding' expected_prompt=$'Read docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md first — it declares typescript-bun.\n\nReview the last commit (git diff HEAD~1) for concern drift.\nThe typescript-bun concern says:\n- Use bun:test, NOT Vitest or Jest\n- Use Biome, NOT ESLint or Prettier\n- Use Bun.serve(), NOT Express or Node http\n\nCheck every file in the diff. Report any imports, tools, or patterns\nthat violate the declared concerns. Be specific about which file and\nline has the drift.\n\nCreate a tracker issue for each drift finding:\nddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding,area:testing' expected_hash='343d7e245e903303'</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh:64
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator hardcodes the expected replay prompt in expected_prompt and only checks that demo.sh still contains the updated ddx command string. It never extracts the full REVIEW_PROMPT block from docs/demos/helix-concerns/demo.sh and compares that live prompt to the recorded fixture. A future edit to any other line in the demo prompt can therefore desynchronize the shipped demo script from docs/demos/helix-concerns/agent-dictionary/343d7e245e903303.json while bash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh still passes.
Suggested fix: Parse the REVIEW_PROMPT block directly from docs/demos/helix-concerns/demo.sh, compare it byte-for-byte to the fixture prompt, and recompute the expected prompt hash and prompt_len from that extracted text so prompt drift anywhere in the heredoc fails deterministically.
notes:
<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-11T13:26:21Z</timestamp>
  <status>PASS</status>
  <acceptance>
    <criterion name='review-prompt-derived-from-demo' status='pass' evidence='tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh now extracts the helix-concerns REVIEW_PROMPT heredoc directly from docs/demos/helix-concerns/demo.sh and derives the fixture hash/path from that live prompt body.'/>
    <criterion name='fixture-payload-validated-against-derived-hash' status='pass' evidence='The validator now checks the matching agent-dictionary/<sha>.json prompt, prompt_hash, and prompt_len against the extracted REVIEW_PROMPT text.'/>
    <criterion name='prompt-drift-fails-without-refresh' status='pass' evidence='If any line in the REVIEW_PROMPT heredoc changes without a refreshed fixture, the derived hash/path lookup or payload comparison fails deterministically.'/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern='demo-asciinema' command='bash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh' status='pass'/>
    <gate concern='diff-hygiene' command='git diff --check' status='pass'/>
  </gates>
  <ratchets/>
</measure-results>

File: workflows/phases/06-iterate/GATE.yaml:38
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 4d9bd29 updates the deploy contract to say HELIX tracker state lives in .ddx/beads.jsonl and that deploy work is managed through ddx bead, but the downstream iterate gate still hard-codes the legacy path .ddx/issues.jsonl as the tracker initialization check. That leaves the deploy→iterate contract internally inconsistent: the same workflow pass now says deploy issues live in .ddx/beads.jsonl while Iterate still validates a file HELIX no longer treats as canonical.
Suggested fix: Update the iterate gate to validate the built-in tracker surface (.ddx/beads.jsonl or an equivalent ddx bead-based check) so downstream phase-entry rules match the deploy and top-level workflow contract.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>tests/validate-skills.sh — A deterministic repo test executes bin/helix without manually pre-setting HELIX_ROOT, verifies the wrapper delegates to scripts/helix via plugin-root resolution, and runs from the normal FEAT-004 plugin-packaging validation surface together with bash tests/validate-skills.sh or bash tests/test-install.sh.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:73
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 783ef571 adds new runtime logic in bin/helix to derive HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or the wrapper path, but the deterministic verification surface still does not execute that wrapper. tests/validate-skills.sh only checks that bin/helix exists and is executable, and tests/test-install.sh exercises the copied HOME/.local/bin/helix launcher with HELIX_ROOT injected manually instead of the plugin wrapper path. That leaves the new delegation logic effectively code-inspection-only: a regression in bin/helix path resolution can slip through the documented plugin packaging lane while FEAT-004 and the implementation plan both require a bin/helix delegation test.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that runs bin/helix through the plugin-style path, proves it sets HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or wrapper resolution, and wire that check into the standard plugin packaging validation lane.
title: correctness: add deterministic coverage for bin/helix delegation

acceptance: A bead whose ADRs are discovered only through secondary matching produces a non-empty <adrs> section with summarized ADR content, and an automated test covers that path.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def summarize_adr(root: Path, ref: str) -> str: path = resolve_artifact_path(root, ref) if path is None or not path.exists(): return "" markdown = read_text(path) adr_id = extract_doc_id(markdown) or strip_markdown(ref) title_match = re.search(r"^#\s+(.*)$", markdown, re.MULTILINE) title = strip_markdown(title_match.group(1)) if title_match else adr_id title = re.sub(rf"^{re.escape(adr_id)}:\s*", "", title) decision = choose_best_clause( iter_markdown_clauses(extract_section(markdown, "Decision")), "decision selected approach", ) rationale = extract_adr_rationale(markdown) summary = f"{adr_id} {title}" if decision: summary += f" — {decision}" if rationale: summary += f" Why: {rationale}" return summary</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:340
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new secondary ADR discovery path is dead code. `secondary_adr_matches()` returns bare filenames like `ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md`, but `build_adrs()` passes those strings to `summarize_adr()`, which calls `resolve_artifact_path()`. That resolver only handles direct repo-relative paths, extracted document IDs, or filename stems, so a bare filename with `.md` never resolves. In the live repo, `secondary_adr_matches()` returns both ADR filenames for a workflow bead, while `build_adrs()` still returns `[]`. That means the FEAT-006 fallback path for ADRs not wired into concerns silently never contributes an `<adrs>` section.
Suggested fix: Make secondary matches return repo-relative paths or ADR document IDs, or teach `resolve_artifact_path()` to resolve bare ADR filenames. Add a test that exercises the secondary-match path end to end so the fallback cannot regress silently.
notes:
<measure-results>

  <acceptance>
    <criterion name="secondary-match ADR summary" status="pass" evidence="scripts/refresh_context_digests.py now emits repo-relative ADR refs from secondary_adr_matches(), so build_adrs() resolves and summarizes fallback matches into <adrs>."/>
    <criterion name="regression coverage" status="pass" evidence="tests/test_refresh_context_digests.py adds an end-to-end case where ADRs are discovered only via secondary matching and verifies the digest includes ADR-888 summary content."/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_refresh_context_digests.py" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="bash tests/validate-context-digests.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="bash tests/helix-cli.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="bash tests/validate-state-rules.sh" status="pass"/>

    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="git diff --check" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="workflow/testing" command="lefthook run pre-commit" status="pass"/>
  </gates>
  <ratchets></ratchets>
</measure-results>
title: correctness: fix secondary ADR discovery in digest helper

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1191
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The runtime path now rejects closed or non-execution-ready selectors, but the dry-run path still prints a successful-looking ddx agent execute-bead <ready-bead> command and exits 0 when selector resolution returned no execution-ready bead. HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 bash scripts/helix build --dry-run helix-9efef95b currently reports a placeholder command instead of surfacing the same rejection that the real execution path emits.
Suggested fix: Validate the resolved issue_id before the dry-run print path, or make the dry-run branch fail when an explicit selector matched no execution-ready bead, and add a deterministic regression test covering build --dry-run <closed-id>.
title: correctness: reject non-ready build selectors in dry-run mode

acceptance: skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md no longer says every issue enters the tracker ready to execute, explicitly distinguishes execution-ready implementation work from planning/review fallback work for vague requests, and bash tests/validate-skills.sh fails if the blanket execution-ready wording returns
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md — If you cannot derive deterministic success criteria from the governing artifacts, do not create a queue-ready implementation bead anyway. Route the work back to planning/polish, or file it as a not-execution-ready planning/review bead with the missing information called out explicitly.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md:9
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The commit adds the fallback rules that block vague build work from entering the execution queue, but the skill still opens with the unconditional statement "Every issue should enter the tracker ready to execute." That conflicts with the new guidance at lines 61-64 and 84-87 telling agents to route under-specified work back to planning/polish or file it as a not-execution-ready planning/review bead. Because prompt openings have outsized influence on agent behavior, the contradictory introduction weakens the intended fix and still nudges triage toward queue-ready build beads for vague requests.
Suggested fix: Rewrite the heading/opening so the skill distinguishes execution-ready implementation beads from planning/review fallback beads, and extend skill validation to fail if the blanket execution-ready claim is reintroduced.
notes:
<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-11T04:35:18Z</timestamp>

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>HELIX should consume DDx substrate capabilities rather than shadow-implement them · Treat execute-bead as the canonical bounded execution primitive when DDx ships it · Prefer queue cleanup that closes already-satisfied findings instead of carrying stale review debt</practices>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 · FEAT-011 · TD-011 — HELIX already specifies ddx agent execute-bead as the bounded DDx-managed execution primitive, but live workflow docs and implementation surfaces still mix that contract with direct ddx agent run orchestration.</governing>
</context-digest>

DDX now ships `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` locally. Align HELIX governing docs and queue policy to that shipped capability, identify the remaining gap between current HELIX implementation and the contract already written in FEAT-011 / TD-011 / CONTRACT-001, and queue any concrete follow-on implementation work needed to start using execute-bead in the HELIX execution path.

Scope:
- Reconcile HELIX governing docs that still describe direct `ddx agent run` orchestration for bounded bead execution.
- Distinguish DDx-managed execute-bead flows from plain `ddx agent run` usage for planning, review, and non-managed prompts.
- Review the outstanding HELIX queue for stale findings already satisfied, findings superseded by execute-bead adoption, and follow-on work that becomes newly relevant once execute-bead is available.
- Create any follow-on build/test beads needed for actual helix-run / helix-build adoption after the contract is aligned.
title: Align HELIX execution contract to shipped DDx execute-loop / execute-bead capabilities

In either case, the deploy artifact metadata and template agree on the required inputs for monitoring-setup generation.</governing>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml:57 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/template.md:62
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 839e82e removes `runbook` from `monitoring-setup.dependencies.requires`, but the artifact contract still assumes the runbook exists before this creation step. `monitoring-setup` remains `creation_order: 2` while `runbook` is `creation_order: 3`, yet the template still requires a `Runbook Links` section and the validation check still asks the document to reference the runbook. That leaves the deploy workflow internally inconsistent: the scheduler can now generate `monitoring-setup` before `runbook`, but the generated document is still asked to link to a later artifact.
Suggested fix: Make the metadata and template agree on whether `runbook` is an input. Either restore a backward-compatible prerequisite by reordering the artifacts, or keep the current ordering and relax the monitoring contract so incident response can be completed without pre-existing runbook links.
title: integration: align monitoring-setup runbook contract with deploy ordering

acceptance: HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.
title: Add first-class contract artifact support in HELIX design phase

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def build_digest( item: dict, principles: list[str], library: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]], root: Path, ) -> tuple[str, list[str]]: existing_digest, body = split_digest(item.get("description", "")) labels = infer_area_labels(item) bead_areas = {label.removeprefix("area:") for label in labels} matched = [ name for name, data in library.items() if concern_matches(bead_areas, data["areas"]) ] adrs = build_adrs(root, item, labels, matched, library) practices = [] for name in matched: practices.extend( select_digest_practices( library[name]["library_practices"], library[name]["override_practices"], limit=2, ) ) extra_tags = extract_extra_tags(existing_digest)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:123
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new select_digest_practices() merge heuristic still drops override-specific guidance when the first override bullet duplicates the first library bullet. The function always appends library_practices[0] and override_practices[0], then deduplicates and truncates to limit. With limit=2, a case like library=[A, B], overrides=[A, C] returns [A, B], so the digest again omits all override-only content even though FEAT-006 and the reviewed bead's acceptance require merged practices to include both baseline and override guidance when overrides exist.
Suggested fix: When overrides are present, select at least one non-duplicate override bullet before truncation. Add a regression test covering a duplicated-first-bullet case so the helper cannot silently regress back to library-only output.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md:58
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The refreshed plan says the current command surface was validated against `bash scripts/helix help`, then lists `input` and groups `commit` as part of that validated surface. But `bash scripts/helix help` does not print an `input` command at all, and its Usage block omits `commit` as an invocable surface even though the dispatcher has `commit)` and `input)` handlers in scripts/helix. That leaves the implementation plan internally inconsistent with the verification source it cites, so operators using the plan cannot tell whether the CLI help is stale or the plan is overstating what the documented command surface actually is.
Suggested fix: Make the implementation plan and `bash scripts/helix help` agree. Either remove commands from the plan until the help text advertises them, or update the help output so every command claimed in the Current Command Surface section is visible in the documented CLI surface.
title: correctness: reconcile implementation-plan command surface with helix help evidence

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>2d2715d77ca54217d9d22312c03a4303ddf513a6</governing>
</context-digest>

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — Commands: run Loop over ready HELIX work, then check whether to continue doctor Check installation health; --fix auto-repairs plugin symlinks and skills start Launch helix run as a background process (PID file written) stop Kill the background helix run cleanly input Accept sparse intent and shape governed HELIX work frame Create or refine product vision, PRD, and feature specs build Run one bounded implementation pass check Run the bounded HELIX queue-health check align Run reconcile-alignment for a scope (default: repo) backfill Run documentation backfill for a scope (default: repo) design Create a comprehensive design document through iterative refinement polish Refine issues before implementation (dedup, coverage, criteria) next Show recommended next issue review Fresh-eyes review of recent work experiment Run one experiment iteration for metric-driven optimization evolve Thread a new requirement through the artifact stack commit Commit with HELIX format, run build gate, push, close issue triage Create a bead via ddx bead create</governing>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:75
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5990eec changes FEAT-011, CONTRACT-001, and TD-011 to reject a separate stage-personality or stage-model configuration surface and to keep stage stance in HELIX prompts while DDx owns concrete model resolution. The shipped wrapper still exposes `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` / `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, documents `--smart-model` / `--cheap-model`, and hard-wires stage families to `resolve_model smart|cheap` across frame/build/review dispatch. The public microsite CLI docs mirror the same contract. That leaves the newly closed design bead internally inconsistent with the shipped runtime and public docs, so the repository still presents stage-authored stance as a first-class model-policy surface.
Suggested fix: Either remove the smart/cheap stage-model override surface from `scripts/helix` and the microsite CLI docs, or explicitly narrow the governing docs to document this surface as an intentional compatibility exception; in either case, the wrapper, public docs, and FEAT-011 / CONTRACT-001 / TD-011 must say the same thing.

Category: correctness

Description: Commit fdc7245 fixed the legacy top-level phase paths, but the repo-specific directory tree still does not match the live `docs/helix` surface in this repository. The tree omits real paths such as `docs/helix/00-discover`, `docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/security-architecture.md`, `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/`, and `docs/helix/03-test/executions/`, while still advertising nonexistent entries such as `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/security.md`, `docs/helix/03-test/test-plan.md`, and `docs/helix/03-test/test-procedures.md`. Because the section is introduced as "In this repository", readers are told this synthetic tree is the live repo layout and can still be sent to files that do not exist.
Suggested fix: Update the repo-specific directory tree and any accompanying artifact summaries so they reflect the real `docs/helix` contents of this repository, or explicitly relabel the section as an illustrative generic example instead of a live repo map.

File: .github/workflows/pages.yml:78
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The Pages workflow still treats public demo recording failures as warnings and keeps the previously committed .cast file. After hx-422fa616 made quickstart, concerns, evolve, and experiment the authoritative shipped public demo inventory, this fallback means CI can silently publish stale demo recordings while the job still passes, so the shipped site can drift from the actual demo scripts.
Suggested fix: Make recording failure fail the workflow for every shipped public demo, or add a required verification step that proves the published .cast files were regenerated successfully for the authoritative inventory before Pages deploy continues.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>b8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96</governing>
</context-digest>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" governing_bead="${2:-}" governing_mode="${3:-}" local governing_note="Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding." if [[ -n "$governing_bead" ]]; then case "$governing_mode" in claimed) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially)." ;; reused) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead)." ;; created) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead)." ;; esac fi cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:931
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 2c4ab51 refreshes reused align beads via refresh_context_digest_for_bead, but it never adds the deterministic scope-derived area labels when the bead already exists. The helper cannot infer repo or directory scopes from a generic align title/description alone: in a scratch repo, a pre-existing open bead titled `align: repo` with labels `helix,phase:review,kind:planning,action:align` remained unlabeled and digest-free after rerunning `helix align repo`. That leaves pre-fix governing beads permanently outside concern matching even on the new reuse path.
Suggested fix: When reusing an existing align bead, explicitly merge the scope-derived labels from `alignment_bead_labels` into the bead before refreshing the digest, or persist equivalent scope-area evidence that the helper can recover deterministically. Add a regression that starts from a pre-fix open align bead and proves `helix align repo` repairs its labels and digest.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — check_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX check action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/check.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead list --json\, \ddx bead blocked --json\. Review scope: ${scope}. CRITICAL: Your output MUST contain the line "NEXT_ACTION: CODE" where CODE is one of BUILD, DESIGN, POLISH, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, or STOP. This line must appear on its own, not inside a code block or backticks. Without this line the automation cannot continue. Output this line FIRST, before any other analysis. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1087
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: run_loop still computes a specific selected_issue and then applies queue-drift checks, epic-focus bookkeeping, acceptance-failure attribution, and post-cycle review to that bead, but run_execute_loop_once delegates with ddx agent execute-loop --once and does not pass any supported selector. The DDx contract only says execute-loop claims the next ready bead, so HELIX can no longer assume DDx executed the same bead that local scheduling selected. If DDx drains a different ready item, HELIX will mark the wrong bead as failed, blocked, reviewed, or promoted. The test shim in tests/helix-cli.sh currently hides this mismatch by reading the private HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE environment variable, which is not part of the documented DDx surface.
Suggested fix: preserve the HELIX-selected bead across the managed path, or consume the actual bead ID/result returned by DDx before any post-cycle bookkeeping. Add a deterministic harness case where DDx drains a different ready bead unless HELIX uses a supported selector/result surface.
title: integration: run loop delegates execute-loop without preserving HELIX-selected issue

<governing>FEAT-005 — Commands: run Loop over ready HELIX work, then check whether to continue doctor Check installation health; --fix auto-repairs plugin symlinks and skills start Launch helix run as a background process (PID file written) stop Kill the background helix run cleanly input Accept sparse intent and shape governed HELIX work frame Create or refine product vision, PRD, and feature specs build Run one bounded implementation pass check Run the bounded HELIX queue-health check align Run reconcile-alignment for a scope (default: repo) backfill Run documentation backfill for a scope (default: repo) design Create a comprehensive design document through iterative refinement polish Refine issues before implementation (dedup, coverage, criteria) next Show recommended next issue review Fresh-eyes review of recent work experiment Run one experiment iteration for metric-driven optimization evolve Thread a new requirement through the artifact stack commit Commit with HELIX format, run build gate, push, close issue triage Create a bead via ddx bead create</governing>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:817
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new align guard rejects every matching `status: in_progress` governing bead without checking whether the claim is stale. In a harness repro that seeds an `align: repo` bead claimed by dead PID `99999`, `helix align repo` now exits with `alignment already active for repo under owner helix (hx-align-stale); refusing duplicate dispatch` instead of reclaiming the stale governing bead and continuing. That leaves the reviewed issue's intended reclaim behavior unresolved for standalone `helix align` runs and any path that reaches `run_alignment()` without the run-loop orphan-recovery prepass.
Suggested fix: Before failing duplicate dispatch, detect whether the in-progress align bead is orphaned (dead or absent claiming PID / beyond the reclaim threshold) and reclaim it through the existing tracker recovery path; keep the hard failure only for live active owners. Add a deterministic regression that covers stale-bead reclaim in addition to the live-owner rejection path.
title: integration: stale in-progress align bead blocks reclaim

Add a boundary contract doc under docs/helix/02-design/contracts/ that explicitly defines:
- DDx-owned substrate responsibilities
- HELIX-owned workflow/methodology responsibilities
- shared integration objects (bead, graph artifact, execution doc/run, metric, ratchet, preserved attempt)
- workflow handoff points where HELIX calls DDx and what evidence it expects back
- anti-goals to prevent scope drift in either repo

Update existing HELIX docs to point to this contract where appropriate.
title: Write HELIX/DDx boundary contract doc

acceptance: Across workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/{deployment-checklist,monitoring-setup,runbook}/meta.yml, every `dependencies.requires[].path` that references another 05-deploy artifact points to an artifact with `workflow.creation_order` less than or equal to the current artifact, and no cycle remains among those three deploy artifacts.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/deployment-checklist/meta.yml:19 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml:23
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 82cf344 removes the runbook's backward outputs, but the 05-deploy graph is still not order-consistent. `deployment-checklist` declares `creation_order: 1` while requiring `monitoring-setup` and `runbook`, and `monitoring-setup` declares `creation_order: 2` while requiring `runbook`. Automation that treats `dependencies.requires` as upstream prerequisites still sees forward edges to later artifacts, so the deploy graph remains cyclic or unschedulable despite the closed bead claiming consistency.
Suggested fix: Reconcile `creation_order` and `dependencies.requires` across deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, and runbook so every deploy-phase prerequisite points only to an earlier-or-equal artifact, or move those references into non-prerequisite relationship fields if they are informational only.

    <criterion name='no cycle remains among deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, and runbook' status='pass' evidence='python3 zero-dependency structural check reported orders {deployment-checklist:1, monitoring-setup:2, runbook:3}, bad_edges=[], cycle=False'/>

acceptance: If the helper remains the documented path, running it on a bead with matched ADR refs and a spec-id produces <adrs> and a governing clause extracted from the referenced artifact rather than only spec-id plus acceptance text. Otherwise, the workflow docs clearly scope the helper as partial and keep full digest assembly requirements elsewhere.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:205
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The refreshed workflow docs now tell triage, evolve, polish, and context-digest assembly to use the repo helper when it exists, but the helper does not implement the required ADR and governing-spec phases of the FEAT-006 assembly algorithm. `build_digest()` never resolves ADR references at all, and `build_governing()` reduces governing context to `spec-id` plus the first acceptance clause instead of loading the referenced artifact and extracting the governing requirement. The live digest on helix-7b3c6980 shows the result: no <adrs> block and only `FEAT-006 — Open beads in scope...` as governing context. That leaves future beads with materially less authority than the workflow contract promises while the docs advertise the helper as the canonical deterministic path.
Suggested fix: Either extend the helper to resolve ADR references and governing clauses from the referenced spec files, or narrow the workflow docs so the helper is explicitly limited to label refresh/basic digest normalization until it implements the full FEAT-006 assembly contract.
title: drift: make digest helper honor ADR and governing-spec assembly requirements

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

acceptance: workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md describes the current deploy artifact contract with `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, and `release-notes`, and no longer instructs operators to create only three deploy artifacts on an as-needed basis.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md:224
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5b99811120e4be20852613452de2e19f55063ebd updates workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md to treat `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, and `release-notes` as the live deploy artifact set, but workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md still tells operators to create only checklists, runbooks, and monitoring configurations as needed. The deploy phase guidance is therefore still internally inconsistent after the README fix: the gate and artifact metadata require four first-class outputs under docs/helix/05-deploy/, while the enforcer continues to advertise the older three-artifact model.
Suggested fix: Update the Deploy Artifacts section in workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md so it names the current four-artifact contract and points operators at the canonical docs/helix/05-deploy/ outputs.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 — helix polish must refresh the context digest against current upstream</governing>
</context-digest>

Repo alignment review area under governing bead helix-93e11c09. Validate context-digest coverage, area-label propagation, and concern-aware re-entry against FEAT-006, concern-resolution, fresh-eyes review, and the live open queue.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:255
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new flagged-fixture check does not actually prove the non-measurable acceptance was flagged for this rule. bash tests/validate-skills.sh stays green even though the flagged fixture invocation prints "validated measurable acceptance on 0 execution-ready bead(s)", because the helper skips execution-eligible=false beads and the test asserts neither a checked count nor a reason tied to non-measurable acceptance. A future regression could change the fixture from "flagged for vague acceptance" to any generic non-executable state and this lane would still pass.
Suggested fix: Make the validation assert the flagged path explicitly, for example by checking for a diagnostic or fixture field that records the bead was marked not execution-ready because of non-measurable acceptance, and fail if the helper reports zero relevant beads without that evidence.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-rules.yml:46
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 27c7051 makes ITERATE require all matching deploy beads to be complete and no open deploy bead to remain, but the surrounding story-state contract still treats active deploy work as `open` only. HELIX claims execution beads by setting `status: in_progress` (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md:173-179, workflows/EXECUTION.md:457, tests/helix-cli.sh:2364). A story with a claimed deploy bead therefore no longer satisfies the updated ITERATE condition, yet the written DEPLOY examples and query hints still talk only about open deploy issues. That leaves the contract without a deterministic phase for stories whose deploy work is actively in progress.
Suggested fix: Update the deploy/iterate state-detection contract so any non-closed matching `phase:deploy` bead, including `in_progress`, keeps the story in DEPLOY. Align workflows/state-rules.yml, workflows/state-machine.yaml, workflows/artifact-hierarchy.md, and tests/validate-state-rules.sh around that tracker-status rule.

acceptance: The HELIX iterate contract defines a deterministic way to prove that story `US-{id}` reached ITERATE. Either canonical iterate output templates include an explicit story-reference field with documented query rules, or the story state machine stops requiring shared iterate docs to reference `US-{id}` and instead uses deploy/tracker evidence that already exists.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-machine.yaml:224
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new contract says a story reaches ITERATE when shared iterate outputs or linked follow-on work reference `US-{id}`, but the canonical iterate artifacts do not define any required story identifier or story-reference field. `metrics-dashboard`, `security-metrics`, and `improvement-backlog` are iteration-wide documents, and their templates currently expose review windows, metrics, recommendations, and tracker refs without any story-scoped key. As written, the new state machine requires story-linked iterate evidence that the canonical iterate outputs are not required to carry, so story-level iterate detection becomes non-deterministic whenever a deploy completes without separate follow-on issues.
Suggested fix: Either add an explicit story-reference contract to the iterate outputs/templates and document how it is queried, or keep story-level iterate detection anchored in deploy/tracker evidence until a deterministic story-keyed iterate artifact exists.

acceptance: ddx bead show helix-8093c918 reports closing_commit_sha=d242771dbfcbb8baf7585f6c49f049b5aad30f13, and automated last-commit review resolution for that bead targets the queue-snapshot implementation commit rather than the unrelated parent tracker-close commit.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:99
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit d242771 closes bead helix-8093c918 in .ddx/beads.jsonl, but the row records closing_commit_sha=5122af50a8015de1f340f84a5965f79f5ba8cf31, which is the unrelated parent commit that closed helix-420f4f76. That leaves the tracker pointing at the wrong implementation diff for helix-8093c918. In the automated review loop, fresh-eyes review prefers a bead's closing_commit_sha when resolving the implementation commit behind tracker-only follow-up commits, so this bad SHA can send later review or archaeology to an unrelated change and break durable evidence for what actually fixed the queue snapshot.
Suggested fix: Update helix-8093c918 so closing_commit_sha records the actual implementation commit d242771dbfcbb8baf7585f6c49f049b5aad30f13, and tighten the closure bookkeeping path so a bead closed in the current commit cannot inherit an unrelated parent SHA.

<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-30814e44</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance: A deterministic repo test fails on a checked-in vague execution-ready acceptance fixture, passes when the fixture is flagged or rejected as not execution-ready, and runs from the standard validation lane together with bash tests/validate-skills.sh
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:207
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The implementation acceptance requires deterministic validation of at least one rejection or flagging path for non-measurable acceptance text, but the new validator only checks for literal substrings in docs and skills. `bash tests/validate-skills.sh` passes as long as the wording remains present, even if `helix-triage` or `helix-polish` never actually reject or reroute a vague execution-ready bead. A regression in the real queue-readiness behavior would therefore ship with a green validation lane because the test never exercises any negative case.
Suggested fix: Add a deterministic fixture-based validation that feeds vague acceptance text such as `works correctly` through the canonical triage/polish validation surface, or a dedicated helper used by those surfaces, and assert that the bead is flagged not execution-ready or rejected.
notes:

    <criterion name='flagged non-execution-ready fixture passes' status='pass' evidence='python3 scripts/validate_execution_ready_beads.py tests/fixtures/execution-ready-beads/flagged-acceptance.jsonl exits 0.'/>
    <criterion name='standard validation lane covers the behavior check' status='pass' evidence='bash tests/validate-skills.sh passes and now runs the helper against both checked-in fixtures.'/>

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — review_prompt() { local scope="${1:-last-commit}" local in_loop="${2:-0}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX fresh-eyes review action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md") Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${in_loop:+This review is running inside the automated helix run loop after an implementation cycle.} IMPORTANT: File each actionable finding (critical, high, medium severity) as a tracker issue with label \review-finding\ so findings are durable and queryable via \ddx bead list --label review-finding\. See the "Filing Findings as Tracker Issues" section in the action for the exact command pattern. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:246
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed commit makes `helix input` the preferred first-class intake surface in FEAT-002, FEAT-011, workflows/EXECUTION.md, and workflows/REFERENCE.md, but the shipped `helix help` output still omits `helix input` entirely and presents `helix run` as the leading surface. Operators following the live CLI help cannot discover the new contract, so the docs-to-CLI boundary remains inconsistent after commit 466924b.
Suggested fix: Add `helix input` to the usage/command help text with its autonomy flag shape, and update the help wording so `helix run` / `helix build` are clearly compatibility wrappers relative to the DDx-managed path. Add deterministic help-output coverage to prevent regression.
notes: Verified during alignment pass helix-93e11c09: bash scripts/helix help now includes helix input and documents DDx-managed execution guidance for helix run / helix build; bash tests/helix-cli.sh passed 126 tests on 2026-04-10.
title: drift: expose helix input in live CLI help

Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The FEAT-007 verification lane is green, but the Playwright suite still does not exercise the user-facing search workflow at all. A text search over website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts shows page-load, navigation, and screenshot coverage only; there are no search input interactions or assertions on result routing. Suggested fix: Add a Playwright scenario that opens site search, queries at least glossary and CLI content, asserts the result URLs are valid, and verifies navigation succeeds when a result is selected.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:170
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The helper now resolves concern metadata and ADR refs only from <library_root>/workflows/concerns (scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:170,381). FEAT-006 still says projects can add custom concerns, but in plugin mode the project root and HELIX library root differ. Reproducing with --root <project> --library-root <plugin> and a concern defined only in <project>/workflows/concerns/custom-cli causes the helper to crash with FileNotFoundError on <library_root>/workflows/concerns/custom-cli/practices.md before any digest can be written. That turns supported project-local concerns into a hard failure for refresh_context_digests.py and any HELIX creation path that shells through it.
Suggested fix: Make concern resolution check project-local workflows/concerns/<name>/ first and fall back to the HELIX library copy, or otherwise merge both sources intentionally, then add a regression test that exercises distinct project-root and library-root paths with a custom concern.
title: integration: support project-local custom concerns during digest refresh
