description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:110
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new digest helper discards library practices whenever a concern has project overrides. `build_concern_library()` uses `overrides.get(name) or parse_practice_bullets(...)`, so every matched concern in this repo contributes only override bullets to the rebuilt digest. FEAT-006 and the context-digest reference require project overrides to be applied on top of library practices, not to replace them entirely. The resulting live digests omit base concern guidance such as library testing and artifact-sync practices, which weakens the self-contained execution context the helper was introduced to restore.
Suggested fix: Merge library practices with project overrides deterministically instead of choosing one or the other. Preserve the core area-matched library practices, then layer repo-specific override bullets on top when assembling the compact digest.

<governing>workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml — A deterministic validation command in the repo fails if a 05-deploy artifact requires a later artifact or if the deploy artifact graph contains a cycle, and the check is wired into the standard validation lane used for workflow-contract changes.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml:11
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The implementation bead for commit 82cf344 was measured with one-file `yq` checks against `runbook/meta.yml`, and the repo has no deterministic validation that checks cross-artifact creation-order consistency for the 05-deploy graph. That gap let the fix pass even though `deployment-checklist` and `monitoring-setup` still require later deploy artifacts. Without a shared validator, future graph edits can again report local success while leaving the canonical deploy contract contradictory.
Suggested fix: Add a deterministic repo validation that loads the 05-deploy artifact metadata together and fails when any `dependencies.requires` edge points to a later artifact or when the deploy artifact graph contains a cycle. Run it in the normal workflow-contract validation lane.

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>.github/workflows/test.yml — run: bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/test.yml:25
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 15f6198 fixes the Pages recording loop in .github/workflows/pages.yml, and local repro confirms the new status-file check blocks copying failed demo casts. But the repository's standard validation lane still never exercises that failure path: .github/workflows/test.yml only runs bash tests/helix-cli.sh, bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh, bash tests/validate-state-rules.sh, bash tests/validate-skills.sh, and bash tests/test-install.sh, and rg confirms none of those suites reference pages.yml, Record demos, or the new status-file contract. That leaves the fix protected only by one-off local repro notes in the commit message, which violates the active demo-asciinema and e2e-playwright expectation that user-facing demo/site workflows have deterministic regression checks rather than manual-only verification.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that simulates the Pages Record demos shell flow for both failing and passing wrapped demo commands, asserts the failing case exits before cast copy, wire that test into the normal repo validation lane, and keep the acceptance aligned with .github/workflows/pages.yml.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml:33
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The restored runbook metadata says the artifact requires `deployment-checklist` and `monitoring-setup`, but it also declares those same peer artifacts as outputs in `dependencies.enables` and `relationships.informs` while `creation_order` is 3. That makes the artifact graph internally contradictory: automation reading `enables` can treat earlier deploy artifacts as newly unlocked after runbook creation even though the runbook itself depends on them.
Suggested fix: Remove backward `enables` and downstream relationship claims for earlier deploy artifacts, or otherwise rewrite the runbook/checklist/monitoring metadata so the dependency graph and creation order are acyclic and consistent.

<governing>FEAT-005 — Options: --agent Agent harness to use: codex, claude, ddx-agent, embedded, or any harness from \ddx agent list\ (default: ${agent}) --claude Shorthand for --agent claude --codex Shorthand for --agent codex --ddx-agent Shorthand for --agent ddx-agent --embedded Shorthand for --agent embedded --forge Deprecated alias for the embedded DDx agent harness --model Explicit model override to pass through to DDx / the harness --effort Reasoning effort: low, medium, high, max (defaults vary by action) --review-every Run alignment every N completed implementation cycles --max-cycles Stop after N completed implementation cycles --no-auto-align Do not auto-run alignment when check returns ALIGN --no-auto-review Do not run fresh-eyes review after each implementation --review-threshold N Skip review for changes below N lines (default: 100) --review-agent Use a different agent for reviews (default: adversarial alternate) --dry-run Print the agent command instead of executing it --quiet, -q Suppress progress output (command echo, prompt sizes) --summary, -s Concise output with log-file line pointers (implies --quiet)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1155
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new generic `HELIX_MODEL` / `--model` surface is forwarded unconditionally to DDx-managed execution in `run_execute_bead` and `run_execute_loop_once` after `managed_build_agent` may switch the harness to the auto-selected alternate agent. In the default codex-primary / claude-alternate setup, `env HELIX_AGENT=codex HELIX_ALT_AGENT=claude HELIX_MODEL=gpt-5.4 bash scripts/helix build --dry-run ...` now emits `--harness claude --model gpt-5.4`, and `helix run --dry-run` emits the same cross-harness pair. That recreates exactly the concrete-model-policy coupling FEAT-011 / CONTRACT-001 / TD-011 were trying to remove and can make the new public override surface fail as soon as adversarial rotation is active.
Suggested fix: Only pass an explicit model override to DDx-managed execution when the execution harness matches the requested primary harness, or otherwise disable/override adversarial rotation when `--model` is supplied. Add deterministic CLI coverage so dry-run output never combines a codex-specific model override with `--harness claude` (or the inverse).

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator accepts any open HELIX bead whose description starts with `Explicit omission rationale:`, even though the FEAT-006 contract only permits that form when a governing workflow explicitly allows digest omission. A temp tracker containing a generic open build bead with `description="Explicit omission rationale: skipped for convenience"` now exits 0 under `python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile>`, and a repo-wide search finds no workflow/action that currently grants that exception. This turns the validator into a bypass: a bead can silently dodge the digest requirement by writing the magic prefix, so missing-digest regressions can pass the gate instead of being surfaced.
Suggested fix: Make omission-rationale acceptance conditional on explicit workflow authorization that the validator can verify deterministically (for example a dedicated label/field set by the allowed creation paths), and add a negative fixture proving a generic HELIX bead with the prefix still fails validation.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>2c4ab51029b99d1c557192d413ac586c3778bff2</governing>
</context-digest>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<governing>pages — for demo in quickstart concerns evolve experiment; do echo "::group::Recording $demo" dir=$(mktemp -d) cast="website/static/demos/helix-${demo}.cast" recorded_cast="${dir}/helix-${demo}.cast" (cd "$dir" && asciinema rec \ --cols 100 --rows 30 \ --command "HELIX_DEMO_RECORDING=1 bash ${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/docs/demos/helix-${demo}/demo.sh" \ "$recorded_cast" 2>&1) test -s "$recorded_cast" cp -f "$recorded_cast" "$cast" echo "✓ $demo recorded ($(wc -c < "$cast") bytes)" echo "::endgroup::" done</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/pages.yml:79
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5b284c8 removed the warning fallback, but the new implementation still does not fail the Pages workflow when a shipped demo command exits non-zero. Local reproduction shows 'asciinema rec --command "bash -lc '\''exit 17'\''"' returns exit code 0 and writes a non-empty cast, so the current step's '(...)', 'test -s', and 'cp -f' sequence still succeeds and copies a cast from a failed demo run.
Suggested fix: Capture the wrapped demo command's exit status explicitly and assert it before accepting the cast, or add a verification mechanism that proves each shipped demo completed successfully before the Pages job continues.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md:82
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The Live Queue Snapshot section says the snapshot was captured after claiming `helix-62559453` and that there was `1` in-progress open bead (`helix-62559453`). But the backlog tables immediately below enumerate 22 issue IDs that exactly match the current post-close open queue and do not include `helix-62559453` at all. The section therefore mixes two different tracker states: a snapshot that still counts the refresh bead as open, and a backlog listing that already excludes it. Because this document is meant to steer implementation ordering from live tracker state, that mismatch can send later runs down the wrong queue or make the counts impossible to audit against `ddx bead status` / `ddx bead list --status open --json`.
Suggested fix: Rebuild the Live Queue Snapshot and backlog tables from one tracker moment and make them agree. If `helix-62559453` is part of the counted open queue, include it or reduce the backlog table count accordingly; otherwise rewrite the prose/counts to the post-close queue state and remove the in-progress claim.

acceptance: HELIX design/docs define an integration contract where HELIX selects stage tier and harness constraints while DDx resolves the concrete model via ddx-agent catalog policy; compatibility for existing HELIX smart/cheap overrides is specified; scripts/helix and tests/helix-cli.sh implementation steps are identified without leaving hardcoded model-version policy in HELIX.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/EXECUTION.md — HELIX design/docs define an integration contract where HELIX selects stage tier and harness constraints while DDx resolves the concrete model via ddx-agent catalog policy</governing>
<evidence>
- scripts/helix currently hardcodes concrete smart/cheap model names in resolve_model(): claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini.
- HELIX docs and help text expose --smart-model / --cheap-model and describe concrete defaults in ways that duplicate DDx policy.
- The desired behavior is: HELIX decides whether a stage needs smart/fast/cheap and which harness family to use; DDx resolves the concrete current model through ddx-agent catalog policy under user constraints.
- This removes split-brain model policy and avoids HELIX drifting behind current recommended models when ddx-agent catalog data changes.
</evidence>
</context-digest>

Design how HELIX should consume DDx tier/model policy backed by ddx-agent's shared model catalog instead of hardcoding concrete model names.

Scope:
- Define the integration contract between HELIX and DDx for tier-based model selection.
- Decide whether HELIX should pass tier names, family aliases, harness constraints, or another structured request to DDx.
- Identify compatibility behavior for existing HELIX_SMART_MODEL / HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL overrides and existing CLI flags.
- Update HELIX execution contract/docs so supervisory stage selection remains in HELIX while concrete model policy is delegated downward through DDx to ddx-agent catalog data.
- Outline the implementation/test plan for scripts/helix and tests/helix-cli.sh.

Notes:
- Keep the responsibility split crisp: HELIX owns stage policy (review/design/align vs build/check/polish), DDx owns concrete harness/model resolution, ddx-agent owns reusable model catalog data.
- The resulting design should avoid reintroducing hardcoded codex/claude model version strings in HELIX.

title: Make HELIX consume DDx tier policy backed by ddx-agent model catalog

acceptance: A deterministic test shows the validator checks the same bead IDs as ddx bead ready --execution for a mixed tracker fixture, excluding closed, deferred, and otherwise non-ready execution-phase beads while still rejecting open execution-ready beads with non-measurable acceptance.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_execution_ready_beads.py:59
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator's execution-ready predicate does not match the canonical queue surface. On the live tracker, running python3 scripts/validate_execution_ready_beads.py .ddx/beads.jsonl inspects 193 beads, including 169 closed beads, while ddx bead ready --execution --json reports 28 execution-ready beads. Because the helper ignores status and queue-readiness semantics, its default path produces noisy failures against historical tracker state instead of validating the same bead set HELIX would actually execute.
Suggested fix: Derive the candidate bead set from ddx bead ready --execution --json or replicate its readiness filters exactly before applying acceptance validation, and add a deterministic fixture that proves closed, deferred, and otherwise non-ready beads are excluded.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-bec526ad</governing>
</context-digest>

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:39 and .ddx/beads.jsonl:60
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Implementation commit 80acf3d95d0818ad367d5bf4df112f812cfb9bb2 writes new <measure-results> blocks on helix-4f9883af and helix-7171e7a1 with timestamps 2026-04-10T12:40:00Z and 2026-04-10T12:42:00Z, but the commit itself is dated 2026-04-10T04:33:55-04:00 (08:33:55Z) and the affected rows have updated_at values 08:31:42Z and 08:35:02Z. The new notes therefore claim UTC times that are about four hours in the future, which misorders tracker evidence and breaks the measure.md contract that <timestamp> records the actual measurement time in UTC.
Suggested fix: Generate <measure-results><timestamp> values in real UTC (or omit the Z suffix when writing local time), then correct the two affected bead notes so their timestamps are chronologically consistent with the commit and updated_at metadata.

<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2320
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 2d2715d changes the explicit-selector managed-execution path to run `resolve_execution_target "$selector" 2>/dev/null || true` before both dry-run and real execution. That swallows resolver failures from `tracker_ready --json --execution` or `ddx jq` and then misreports the outcome as `helix: no execution-ready bead matched the build selector`. Reproduction from this review: create a temp `.ddx/beads.jsonl` containing malformed JSON, then run `HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 DDX_BEAD_DIR=<temp> bash scripts/helix build --dry-run hx-bad` or `... build hx-bad`; both exit 1 with the selector-miss message instead of surfacing the tracker parse failure.
Suggested fix: Preserve real resolver failures for explicit selectors by distinguishing "no match" from "resolution failed". Only emit the selector-miss summary when resolution succeeded and returned no issue, and add deterministic coverage for malformed-tracker or resolver-failure cases on both dry-run and real execution paths.
title: integration: preserve tracker errors when explicit build selector resolution fails

Create the initial CRM product framing artifacts from the sparse request "design a CRM".

Assumptions to make explicit in the resulting artifacts unless superseded:
- Primary users: sales reps, sales managers, and CRM admins
- Core workflows: managing contacts/accounts, qualifying leads, tracking opportunities, logging activities, and reviewing pipeline/reporting
- Delivery model: browser-based SaaS with authenticated multi-user access

This bead establishes the first authoritative CRM artifacts so later design and build work have a governing source.

<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.workflow — HELIX enforces Test-Driven Development (TDD) through a structured phase approach where tests are written BEFORE implementation. This ensures quality is built-in from the start and specifications are executable. Human creativity and AI capabilities collaborate throughout, with tests serving as the contract between design and implementation.</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance: In an automated run-loop cycle that creates a tracker-closure sync commit after implementation, the post-implementation review threshold and review scope still inspect the implementation diff rather than the closure-sync diff; deterministic tests cover the commit-selection behavior and pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2936
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: In the automated run loop, post-implementation review decides whether to run and what to review from `git diff HEAD~1`, then invokes `run_review last-commit`. In this repository the most recent commit after the implementation cycle is `91860b8` (`helix-c0d6bdf3 sync tracker closure`), which changes only `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. The actual implementation diff is one commit earlier at `d6da2d5`. This means the auto-review threshold can undercount the work as a tiny tracker-only change and skip review, or review only bookkeeping instead of the implementation that just ran. The action contract at `workflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md:39` and the operator docs at `workflows/EXECUTION.md:604` reinforce the same incorrect `last-commit` assumption for this in-loop context.
Suggested fix: Thread the executed issue ID or implementation commit SHA through the run loop and make auto-review inspect that implementation diff instead of raw HEAD~1 when a tracker close sync commit exists. Update the fresh-eyes review action and execution docs to describe the in-loop behavior explicitly, and add deterministic coverage showing review still targets the implementation commit after a tracker-closure sync commit.
title: integration: review last-commit should target implementation diff, not tracker closure sync

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-stacks-practices-context-digest.md:223
Category: correctness
Severity: important
Description: The design says concerns reference the ADRs that justified them, but library concern files are shared and the project concerns example records only names/categories. That leaves no project-level place to store the ADR that selected an unmodified library concern, so the Spike/POC -> ADR -> Concern -> Context Digest chain is incomplete.
Suggested fix: Extend the project concerns manifest so each selected concern can carry ADR references and any project-specific rationale links, not only overrides.
title: traceability: add project-level ADR refs for selected concerns

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-d2146c63</governing>
</context-digest>

<governing>tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh — assert_contains \ "$demo_script" \ "$expected_command" \ "helix-concerns demo should file review findings with an area label" assert_fixture_matches_demo_prompt "$demo_script" "$fixture_dir" assert_not_contains \ "$demo_script" \ "$stale_command" \ "helix-concerns demo should not retain the stale unlabeled review-finding command"</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh:98
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated validator extracts the helix-concerns REVIEW_PROMPT body and matches the fixture payload against that extracted text, but the semantic checks for the required labeled ddx command still run against the entire demo.sh file via assert_contains/assert_not_contains. If the same command string appears in a comment, another prompt, or unrelated helper text, bash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh can still pass even when the REVIEW_PROMPT itself drops the area-labeled command or retains the stale unlabeled command.
Suggested fix: Bind the expected_command and stale_command assertions to the extracted REVIEW_PROMPT text inside assert_fixture_matches_demo_prompt (or return the extracted prompt and assert against that value) so the validator enforces the reviewed prompt semantics rather than any unrelated occurrence elsewhere in demo.sh.
title: correctness: bind demo command checks to extracted review prompt

acceptance: `website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md` no longer says deploy artifacts are created only "as needed", and the Deploy section contains dedicated entries for `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, and `release-notes` that match the current deploy contract.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md:251
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit b148a319 makes `release-notes` a required deploy exit artifact and therefore reinforces the live four-artifact deploy contract (`deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, `release-notes`). The microsite artifact glossary still says deploy artifacts are created only "as needed" and lists examples rather than the current first-class contract, and it never documents `monitoring-setup` as its own artifact entry at all. Under the active `hugo-hextra` concern, governing artifact changes that are surfaced on the microsite must update the corresponding microsite pages in the same pass; this page still advertises the pre-restoration deploy model.
Suggested fix: Update the Deploy section of `website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md` so it describes the current deploy artifact contract explicitly and add a dedicated `monitoring-setup` entry alongside `deployment-checklist`, `runbook`, and `release-notes`.

description: The helix CLI hard-codes agent harness names and model lists. It should instead call ddx agent list and ddx agent capabilities to discover available harnesses and models at runtime. Also add ddx-agent as a supported HELIX surface alongside codex and claude, normalizing to the DDx harness name `agent` and dispatching via `ddx agent run --harness agent`. title: Support ddx-agent as a HELIX agent and use ddx agent for discovery instead of hard-coding

The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:971-975 and scripts/helix:3004-3016
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new reclaim helper refactor now decides reclaimability from a single tracker snapshot and then mutates tracker state without re-reading the bead immediately before the update. In ensure_alignment_bead(), a matching align bead can be closed after line 965 but before lines 974-975, and HELIX will still unclaim and re-claim it, reopening a bead that another actor just completed and dispatching duplicate alignment work. In recover_orphaned_issues(), the same stale-snapshot pattern at lines 3006-3016 can clear claim metadata on a bead that was closed after the initial read, erasing closure evidence instead of skipping it. This violates the repo’s concurrency model in ADR-001, which requires helix-run to treat tracker changes as live control input at safe boundaries.
Suggested fix: Re-read the live bead state immediately before every reclaiming tracker mutation, and skip reclaim when the bead is no longer in_progress. Add deterministic CLI coverage that simulates a bead closing after the initial snapshot so align/orphan recovery leave the closed bead untouched and do not dispatch duplicate work.
title: integration: reclaim paths act on stale tracker snapshots

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-002 — Running helix triage produces tracker-valid issues with required labels,</governing>
</context-digest>

The design must keep the boundary crisp: DDx owns harness/model execution, while HELIX may still own prompt stance, critique style, risk posture, and stage-specific instructions.

<governing>HELIX should not force DDX_BEAD_PREFIX=hx inside scripts/helix. DDx already derives the correct bead prefix from repo configuration or repo name, and overriding it causes HELIX to look at the wrong issue namespace inside the helix repo.</governing>

- helix run is selecting hx-prefixed historical issues instead of the current helix-prefixed FEAT-011 queue.

Remove the hard-coded DDX_BEAD_PREFIX export so HELIX reads the current repo's real tracker namespace.

- add deterministic coverage that helix reads the helix-prefixed queue in this repo instead of the historical hx-prefixed namespace
- re-run the relevant wrapper tests and restart the Claude worker against the corrected queue

The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def infer_area_labels(item: dict) -> list[str]: labels = set(label for label in item.get("labels", []) if label.startswith("area:")) title = item.get("title", "").lower() for path in bead_paths(item): normalized = path.lower() if normalized.startswith("website/"): labels.add("area:site") if normalized.startswith("docs/demos/"): labels.add("area:demo") if normalized.startswith("docs/"): labels.add("area:docs") if normalized.startswith("tests/"): labels.add("area:testing") if normalized.startswith("scripts/") or normalized.startswith("bin/"): labels.add("area:cli") if normalized.startswith("workflows/") or normalized.startswith(".ddx/"): labels.add("area:workflow") if "/artifacts/" in normalized or normalized.endswith("/artifacts"): labels.add("area:artifacts") if "playwright coverage" in title or "deterministic coverage" in title or "deterministic verification" in title: labels.add("area:testing") if "helix-cli.sh hang" in title: labels.add("area:testing") if "deleted artifact type" in title or "artifact order consistency" in title: labels.add("area:artifacts") return sorted(labels)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:433
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed fix updates `infer_area_labels()` to treat paths ending in `/artifacts` as artifact scope, but `bead_paths()` still never extracts extensionless directory scopes such as `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts`. In a direct helper repro, an align bead or finding that references only that directory path returns `bead_paths=[]` and `infer_area_labels=[]`, so digest refresh still cannot reconstruct `area:workflow` or `area:artifacts` once those labels are missing. The new CLI test only covers wrapper-created labels and does not exercise helper-only inference, so this regression remains undetected.
Suggested fix: Teach `bead_paths()` to recognize extensionless HELIX artifact-directory paths (or otherwise feed raw `spec-id` or scope directory values into area inference), then add deterministic coverage that fails when `refresh_context_digests.py` cannot recover workflow and artifact labels for a `workflows/.../artifacts` scope.
title: correctness: restore helper inference for extensionless artifact scopes

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/assets/json/search-data.json:35
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new repo-owned search-data override preserves a broken synthetic glossary route. After running the Hugo build in website/, the rendered public/en.search-data.json contains both /docs/glossary/ and a second /glossary record, even though the actual page lives only at /docs/glossary/. Hextra's search client uses those JSON keys directly as result routes, so searching for glossary content can surface a non-existent destination. This is now HELIX-owned behavior because commit e61a4f6 shadows the upstream template into website/assets/json/search-data.json.
Suggested fix: Build the synthetic glossary record from the real glossary page permalink instead of relLangURL "glossary", and add a deterministic assertion that the rendered search index contains no nonexistent glossary route.

    <criterion name='search result navigates to a live page' status='pass' evidence='cd website && npx playwright test e2e/microsite.spec.ts passes 17 tests, including Search Workflows coverage that opens site search, queries glossary content, and navigates to the glossary page.'/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern='hugo-hextra' command='cd website && hugo --gc --minify' status='pass'/>
    <gate concern='e2e-playwright' command='cd website && npx playwright test e2e/microsite.spec.ts' status='pass'/>
    <gate concern='repo-hygiene' command='git diff --check' status='pass'/>
  </gates>
  <notes>The search-data template already resolves glossary entries through the real glossary page permalink on the current branch; this pass adds a deterministic Playwright assertion against the rendered search index so the broken synthetic route cannot regress silently.</notes>
</measure-results>
title: correctness: search index emits broken glossary route

<governing>FEAT-005 — check_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX check action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/check.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead list --json\, \ddx bead blocked --json\. Review scope: ${scope}. CRITICAL: Your output MUST contain the line "NEXT_ACTION: CODE" where CODE is one of BUILD, DESIGN, POLISH, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, or STOP. This line must appear on its own, not inside a code block or backticks. Without this line the automation cannot continue. Output this line FIRST, before any other analysis. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2518
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `sync_issue_closing_commit_sha()` mutates the tracker with `tracker_update` and then runs `git commit --only -qm` without checking that commit's exit status before printing success. Both current callers invoke the helper as `sync_issue_closing_commit_sha ... || true` (`scripts/helix:2537` and `scripts/helix:3386`), and Bash disables `set -e` inside a function executed in an `||` list. A minimal reproduction, `bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; f(){ false; echo after; }; f || true; echo survived'`, prints `after` and `survived`, proving the helper can continue after an inner failure. If the repo-local tracker sync commit fails (for example because git identity is missing or the commit is otherwise rejected), HELIX can leave an uncommitted `closing_commit_sha` mutation in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` while still proceeding to review or the next loop step as though the sync succeeded.
Suggested fix: Treat the repo-local tracker sync commit as a required step: check the `git commit` exit status explicitly, return non-zero on failure, and either roll back the tracker mutation or stop the review/run path before continuing. Add deterministic regression coverage for a sync-commit failure path so the loop cannot proceed with a dirty tracker worktree.
title: correctness: sync closing_commit_sha must not continue after tracker-sync commit failure

<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>253d14f4611502d1986a2bd5699c3329f7c17c19</governing>

Description: The revised triage skill now tells agents to write deterministic acceptance and says queue-drained execution beads should prefer exact commands, files, and end-state conditions, but it still does not define the same negative path that polish now has when the governing artifacts do not support a measurable execution contract. workflows/EXECUTION.md:606-608 says beads that cannot be closed from explicit evidence should be refined by helix polish or helix triage before entering the execution queue. Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:379
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated select_digest_practices() helper only avoids duplicates against the already-prioritized prefix, so it still drops override-specific guidance when an early override duplicates a later library bullet. For example, select_digest_practices(['Library practice A', 'Library practice B'], ['Library practice B', 'Override practice C'], 2) currently returns ['Library practice A', 'Library practice B'] instead of preserving any override-only practice. That still violates the merged-practices contract for context digests when overrides exist.
Suggested fix: Choose the prioritized override from override bullets that are not present anywhere in library_practices before falling back to a duplicate, and add a regression test for the later-library-duplicate case so the digest always retains one override-specific practice when available.

acceptance: helix align repo does not dispatch when the only matching governing bead is in_progress under another owner; a stale/open bead is reclaimed or a new relevant open bead is created according to the action contract
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>FEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" governing_bead="${2:-}" governing_mode="${3:-}" local governing_note="Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding." if [[ -n "$governing_bead" ]]; then case "$governing_mode" in claimed) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially)." ;; reused) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead)." ;; created) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead)." ;; esac fi cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:789
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: find_alignment_bead selects any non-closed align bead, and ensure_alignment_bead treats an existing in-progress bead as reusable without reclaiming it. A second helix align run can therefore proceed under a bead still owned by another actor or left stale after a crash, which violates the action contract to search open beads and claim the governing bead before continuing.
Suggested fix: Restrict lookup to status=open for reusable candidates, or explicitly detect in-progress beads and reclaim or fail with a tracker-visible error instead of silently reusing them.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-7abd2548</governing>
</context-digest>

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/validate_context_digests.py — if missing_context or unauthorized_omission or missing_review_area: for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_context: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} is missing " f"<context-digest> or workflow-authorized explicit omission rationale: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in unauthorized_omission: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} uses an explicit omission rationale " f"without {AUTHORIZED_OMISSION_LABEL}: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_review_area: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: review-finding bead {bead_id} is missing area:* label(s): {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) print( "validated context digests / workflow-authorized omission rationales on 0 bead(s): " f"{len(missing_context)} missing digest-or-rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(unauthorized_omission)} unauthorized omission rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(missing_review_area)} review-finding bead(s) missing area labels", file=sys.stderr, ) return 1</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:29
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The validator now treats `digest:omission-authorized` as sufficient proof that a bead came from a workflow-authorized omission path, but this commit never introduces any concrete authorized omission path or provenance the validator can verify. A repo-wide search after `cc8c280` finds only the validator, docs, and the new test fixture mentioning the label; there is still no specific workflow that grants omission for a named bead class. In a temp tracker containing only `labels=["helix","phase:build","digest:omission-authorized"]` and `description="Explicit omission rationale: skipped for convenience"`, `python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile>` exits 0, so any bead can still bypass the digest requirement by self-applying the label.
Suggested fix: Replace the self-attested label check with deterministic authorization the validator can verify from a real, named omission path (for example an allowlisted workflow/type combination or dedicated machine-set field), update the governing workflow docs to define that path explicitly, and add a regression test showing a label-only bead still fails while a genuinely authorized omission path passes.
title: correctness: omission-authorization label is still self-attested

<governing>helix.workflow.execution — Inside helix run, the post-implementation review target is resolved from the executed bead first. When the implementation pass closes the bead and a tracker-sync commit lands after the code commit, the loop reviews the bead's closing_commit_sha instead of raw HEAD~1, so the threshold and review scope still inspect the implementation diff rather than the tracker bookkeeping diff.</governing>

Review finding from reconcile-alignment.
Scope: repo public tracker contract surfaces
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The live wrapper resolves the built-in tracker from .ddx by default (`tracker_dir="${DDX_BEAD_DIR:-${HELIX_TRACKER_DIR:-${target_root}/.ddx}}"`), but the shipped help text and execution guide still say `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` defaults to `.helix/`. description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-007 — HELIX needs a public-facing documentation site and scripted demo reels that show users what HELIX does, how it works, and how to get started. The microsite is the primary onboarding surface — it must take a new user from "what is this?" to running helix run on their own project.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review repo-scope public documentation, microsite, and demo surfaces against the governing workflow contract and active concerns. Focus on onboarding/default-path wording, recording freshness, demo fixtures, and deterministic public-surface validation.

Remaining coverage is helix-08ce87ef and helix-495f0d72. Consolidated report: docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-11-repo.md.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/05-deploy/monitoring-setup.md:28,37
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 417e434 updates `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/{meta.yml,template.md}` so `monitoring-setup` no longer requires a pre-existing runbook: the validation now asks for escalation ownership and only links a runbook once one exists, and the template makes runbook links optional. The canonical deploy doc at `docs/helix/05-deploy/monitoring-setup.md` still lists `runbook escalation paths` as a canonical input and tells authors to link incident-response expectations back to the runbook, which preserves the old prerequisite semantics. That leaves the durable docs out of sync with the live artifact contract reviewed in this pass.
Suggested fix: Update `docs/helix/05-deploy/monitoring-setup.md` so its canonical inputs and minimum prompt bar match the current artifact contract: require operator ownership, routing, and immediate containment guidance up front, and describe runbook links as optional follow-on references once the runbook exists.

Description: Commit a95e276 routes the deleted launch-checklist's cross-functional coordination into linked phase:deploy tracker issues, but HELIX still defines phase:deploy as story-level rollout work. workflows/README.md:212-213 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md:101-107 both describe deploy issues as story-scoped. Either document that phase:deploy issues may also represent release-scoped coordination/owner/dependency work, or revise the launch-checklist replacement mapping to point at a canonical surface that already exists for cross-functional release coordination.

acceptance: Running `helix align workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts` creates or reuses a governing bead labeled with both `area:workflow` and `area:artifacts`, its digest or description reflects both scope areas, and `bash tests/helix-cli.sh` fails if the artifact-directory scope loses `area:artifacts`.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — review_prompt() { local scope="${1:-last-commit}" local in_loop="${2:-0}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX fresh-eyes review action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md") Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${in_loop:+This review is running inside the automated helix run loop after an implementation cycle.} IMPORTANT: File each actionable finding (critical, high, medium severity) as a tracker issue with label \review-finding\ plus at least one scope-appropriate \area:*\ label so findings are durable, concern-aware, and queryable via \ddx bead list --label review-finding\. See the "Filing Findings as Tracker Issues" section in the action for the exact command pattern. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:784
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `alignment_area_labels_from_path()` only emits `area:artifacts` when the scope string contains `/artifacts/`. A directory scope that ends exactly at the artifacts root, such as `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts`, misses that label entirely. In a scratch run, `helix align workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts` created a governing bead with only `area:workflow`, and the stored description rendered `Scope areas: workflow.` That means artifact-directory alignment passes still lose the artifact concern surface this commit was supposed to restore.
Suggested fix: Treat paths whose final segment is `artifacts` as artifact scope in both wrapper-side label seeding and any helper-side inference used for digest refresh, then add deterministic coverage for directory-scoped align targets.
notes:
<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-11T12:29:40Z</timestamp>
  <status>PASS</status>
  <acceptance>
    <criterion name="artifact-directory align bead keeps workflow and artifact scope" status="pass" evidence="scripts/helix now treats paths ending in /artifacts as artifact scope, so helix align workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts seeds both area:workflow and area:artifacts."/>
    <criterion name="digest inference matches wrapper scope inference" status="pass" evidence="scripts/refresh_context_digests.py now infers area:artifacts for paths ending exactly with /artifacts, keeping digest refresh aligned with wrapper labels."/>
    <criterion name="deterministic regression coverage exists" status="pass" evidence="tests/helix-cli.sh adds test_align_artifact_directory_scope_preserves_artifact_area_label to fail if the artifact-directory scope drops area:artifacts or omits both scope areas from the governing bead description."/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern="cli-workflow" command="bash tests/helix-cli.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="context-digests" command="bash tests/validate-context-digests.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="git-diff" command="git diff --check" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="pre-commit" command="lefthook run pre-commit" status="pass"/>
  </gates>
  <commits>
    <commit role="implementation" sha="b8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96"/>
  </commits>
</measure-results>
title: correctness: tag artifact-directory align scopes with area:artifacts

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/README.md:210
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The "Using Helix Phases in Projects" section still prescribes a `docs/helix-phases/` layout and `ddx apply workflows/helix/phases/...` commands, but this repository and the active workflow contract use `docs/helix/` and `workflows/phases/...`. The example structure and template commands therefore point to directories that do not exist, so a user following the README will fail to locate the documented paths or run the documented commands.
Suggested fix: Update the project-convention section to describe the supported `docs/helix/` layout and valid `workflows/phases/...` artifact paths or replace the commands with the current supported workflow entrypoints.
title: correctness: replace stale helix-phases and ddx apply paths in docs README

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:3
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The automated review correctly resolved `last-commit` to implementation commit `3bc47865145f9256c8297021830133061e754e3f` because `HEAD` is the tracker-only close commit `ce7b4f54aaa7388b548cb32e4b54626325adcb18`, but the live `helix-0596f6b0` tracker row still has `closing_commit_sha: null`. That leaves the closed execution bead without the durable implementation-commit pointer that the close commit message says it persisted, so subsequent review/report passes must recover the true implementation commit from git history instead of the bead itself.
Suggested fix: Update `helix-0596f6b0` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` so `closing_commit_sha` is set to `3bc47865145f9256c8297021830133061e754e3f` when the bead is closed, keeping the tracker row self-contained for later review and report actions.

Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2388
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit ae4c544 unconditionally runs `git add "$tracker_file" && git commit ...` after `tracker_close`. That works when the built-in tracker lives under the repo, but HELIX still accepts `DDX_BEAD_DIR` / `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` overrides. When the tracker directory is external, `git add` fails with `is outside repository` after the implementation commit has already been created. Reproduction: in a temp repo with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` pointing at a sibling directory, `bash scripts/helix commit hx-ext-0` exits 128 after creating the implementation commit, leaves the bead closed only in the external tracker file, and never reaches push/reporting.
Suggested fix: Gate the tracker-sync commit on whether `$tracker_file` is inside `$target_root`. If the tracker is external, still sync `closing_commit_sha` in the tracker, but skip the repo-local tracker commit or use a different persistence path that does not require `git add` on an out-of-repo file. Add a deterministic harness test covering external `DDX_BEAD_DIR` mode.
title: integration: keep helix commit working with external tracker dirs
