description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>website/content/docs/getting-started.md — HELIX adds the methodology on top — the development phases (Frame → Design → Test → Build → Deploy → Iterate), the authority order that resolves conflicts between artifacts, the bounded execution loop that decides what to do next, and the skills that turn all of this into agent instructions. You install DDx first, then install HELIX as a DDx package.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/content/docs/getting-started.md:90
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 466924b changes the governing contract to say new docs, quickstarts, plugin docs, and demos should prefer `helix input` plus `ddx agent execute-loop`, but the public surfaces still teach the legacy path: README.md starts with `/helix-run`, website/content/docs/getting-started.md and website/content/docs/cli/_index.md present `helix run` as the primary entrypoint, website/content/docs/demos/_index.md still describes demos as `ddx agent run` driven, and docs/demos/helix-quickstart/README.md keeps the old frame/design/build/review narrative. This violates the active hugo-hextra sync requirement that workflow-rule changes update the corresponding microsite pages in the same pass, leaving public guidance on the superseded execution path.
Suggested fix: Update README, the microsite Getting Started / CLI / Demos pages, and the quickstart demo docs or script so they present `helix input` plus `ddx agent execute-loop` as the default path while labeling `helix run` / `helix build` as compatibility wrappers.
title: drift: update public quickstarts and demos for DDx queue-drain default

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:47
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator rejects every open HELIX bead whose description lacks a <context-digest> block, but the updated FEAT-006 contract and workflows/actions/input.md:84-85 explicitly allow an open bead to carry an explicit omission rationale when the contract permits omission. Reproducing against a temp tracker entry whose description is 'Explicit omission rationale: ...' exits 1 and reports the bead as invalid. That means the new validation gate can fail on queue entries that satisfy the documented omission path instead of the missing-digest failure mode it is supposed to catch.
Suggested fix: Teach the validator to recognize the documented explicit-omission-rationale path, define the allowed marker/format in the contract if needed, and add deterministic fixtures that prove rationale-bearing beads pass while truly missing digests still fail.
title: correctness: allow explicit digest-omission rationale in validator

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation:
- Core intent: create the governed planning/design work needed for a new CRM product.
- Artifact layer affected: frame and design, with downstream decomposition into execution-ready work.
- Explicit constraints: none beyond high autonomy.

Current repo search found no existing CRM artifact stack or matching open bead. The HELIX repo's governing PRD is about HELIX itself, so a separate escalation bead records the repo-scope conflict. Until that is resolved, treat this as greenfield CRM planning work managed through HELIX.

Working assumptions for downstream beads:
- B2B web CRM for a small-to-mid-size sales team
- MVP includes contacts, accounts, leads/opportunities, tasks/activities, notes, pipeline visibility, and basic reporting
- Multi-user SaaS posture; implementation architecture still to be designed
title: Design a CRM

Clarify the distinction between the bead as intended transformation, the execution run as attempt/evidence record, the execution outcome as merged/preserved/blocked/etc., and the realized delta between W and W'. Thread this into the governing HELIX docs where future design and execution work will discover it naturally.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1031
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: resolve_execution_target returns any bead whose ID exists in the tracker before consulting the execution-ready queue. That means helix build <bead-id> will dispatch DDx managed execution for closed, blocked, or otherwise non-execution-ready beads, even though the compatibility contract says build should resolve a matching ready bead before calling ddx agent execute-bead. In this repository, HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 bash scripts/helix build helix-9efef95b attempted to execute the already-closed bead helix-9efef95b instead of rejecting it.
Suggested fix: resolve explicit selectors against ddx bead ready --execution output, or re-check status and execution-eligible fields after lookup, and fail before DDx dispatch when the selector is not currently execution-ready.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/references/context-digest.md:45
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: The digest assembly algorithm selects ADRs by spec-id/topic heuristics instead of starting from the ADRs explicitly referenced by matched concerns and project overrides. That breaks the documented Spike/POC -> ADR -> Concern -> Context Digest chain and can omit the rationale that FEAT-006 requires to be injected.
Suggested fix: Update the assembly contract so digest ADR discovery starts from matched concern/project-override ADR refs, then optionally unions in spec-id-related ADRs as secondary context. Add the missing project-level storage for concern-selection ADR refs if needed.
title: correctness: make context-digest ADR selection follow concern references

<governing>FEAT-011 — DDx preserve outcomes from bounded execution attempts (for example failed required executions or ratchet regressions) are not physics-level conflicts. They terminate the current DDx-managed attempt and hand control back to HELIX for escalation, follow-on beads, or user input.</governing>

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation at autonomy=medium: the user wants HELIX to seed governed planning work for a new CRM product design effort.

Conflict detected during artifact traversal: this repository's higher-authority frame artifacts define HELIX itself as the product (`docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md`, `workflows/README.md`). Creating CRM vision/PRD/design artifacts directly in this repo would therefore contradict the current product scope unless the request is intended as one of the following:
- a separate CRM project/repository that should use HELIX
- a HELIX example/demo artifact pack about CRM design
- a reinterpretation into HELIX product work rather than a CRM product

Deterministic next step: obtain the target-project decision before creating downstream CRM frame or design beads.
notes: Conflict evidence: `docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md` states HELIX is a development control system for AI-assisted software delivery, and `workflows/README.md` defines this repository as the HELIX workflow contract. CRM product artifacts would need a user-confirmed target before downstream frame/design work can be created non-conflictingly at medium autonomy.
title: Clarify CRM intake target before creating frame/design artifacts

acceptance: Run helix run --agent claude --summary --max-cycles 3 against ddx repo with 3 ready beads. After completion: all 3 beads remain closed. No bead reverts to open. Blocker report shows 0 intractable issues.
description: helix run closes beads via the agent, but between cycles beads revert from closed to open. The ddx bead JSONL store locking is sound (chaos tests prove it). The bug is in scripts/helix run loop: after the agent closes a bead and commits, the next cycle startup reclaims or reopens it. Likely cause: the run loop reads beads.jsonl, caches bead state, dispatches agent, then writes back the stale cached state — clobbering the agent's close. Fix: re-read beads.jsonl fresh after each agent call returns. Never cache bead state across cycle boundaries.
title: Fix helix run bead-state-drift: closed beads revert to open between cycles

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:22
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Tracker-only commit d5f4cfa ("helix-1356be0a: record measure and close bead") closed helix-1356be0a and preserved its <measure-results>, but ddx bead show helix-1356be0a --json still returns an empty closing_commit_sha even though the implementation commit is a2143e77f9fd0b0200e7351a36c9800b92373af2. The automated fresh-eyes-review action explicitly prefers an executed bead's closing_commit_sha when tracker bookkeeping lands after the code commit; without it, the run loop has to fall back to git archaeology and can target the wrong diff. Existing helix commit code and deterministic tests already treat this field as required, so the current measure/report close path has regressed relative to the workflow contract.
Suggested fix: Update the measure/report tracker-close path so tracker-only close commits preserve the executed implementation SHA on the bead, repair helix-1356be0a, and add deterministic coverage for the exact record-measure-and-close path that produced d5f4cfa.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py — def build_surface_specs(args: argparse.Namespace) -> list[SurfaceSpec]: return [ SurfaceSpec( label="deploy README", path=args.deploy_readme, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but current HELIX still " "treats four deploy surfaces as first-class in the live contract: " "deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, runbook, and " "release-notes." ), ), SurfaceSpec( label="deploy enforcer", path=args.deploy_enforcer, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but HELIX treats four " "deploy outputs as the live contract under " "docs/helix/05-deploy/:" ), ), SurfaceSpec( label="artifacts glossary", path=args.glossary, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts cover four first-class surfaces in the current " "HELIX contract: deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, " "runbook, and release-notes." ), ), ]</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py:69
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: validate_surface() only checks that each surface still contains the canonical four-artifact fragment and that all four artifact names appear somewhere in the file. If stale three-artifact wording is reintroduced elsewhere in the same document while the canonical paragraph remains intact, the validator still exits 0. Reproduction from this review: copy workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md, append "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but current HELIX still treats three deploy surfaces as first-class in the live contract: `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, and `runbook`." to the copy, then run python3 scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py --deploy-readme <copy> --deploy-enforcer <enforcer> --glossary <glossary>; it prints "validated deploy contract docs: 3 surfaces" and exits successfully. That violates helix-39fc1526 acceptance, which requires the deterministic lane to fail when any reviewed surface reintroduces stale three-artifact wording.
Suggested fix: Extend the docs validator and its regression lane to detect stale three-artifact wording even when the canonical paragraph is still present elsewhere, so contradictory deploy-contract text cannot slip through by coexistence.
title: correctness: reject stale three-artifact wording alongside canonical deploy docs

description:
condense_codex_output() in scripts/helix (lines ~525-620) is a bash reimplementation of output filtering that belongs in DDx's agent service (ddx-205af2f4). Once DDx implements the output condensing filter in ddx exec run, HELIX should:

1. Remove condense_codex_output() from scripts/helix entirely
2. Rely on ddx exec run's condensed output instead of filtering in bash
3. Update run_agent_prompt() codex branch to consume DDx-filtered output
4. Update tests that depend on condense_codex_output behavior

The current bash filter keeps: helix: progress lines, $ tool calls, errors, issue IDs, markdown headings/tables, phase markers. Drops: raw diffs, codex boilerplate, bulk verbose output. This logic should move to DDx.

Depends on: ddx-205af2f4 (DDx agent output condensing filter)

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 — HELIX-authored execution beads must make success machine-auditable. In practice that means deterministic acceptance and success-measurement criteria: exact commands, named checks, concrete files or fields to inspect, and observable end states. Vague success text such as "works correctly" is not a sufficient contract for ddx agent execute-bead or ddx agent execute-loop.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review the HELIX to DDx execution boundary, including execute-loop bookkeeping, explicit selector resolution, and deterministic wrapper proof coverage.
title: Alignment review: repo - DDx managed execution delegation

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:94
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validate_helix_triage_intro regex only matches blanket priming when the quantifier is followed immediately by a bare noun like issue/issues/bead/beads/task/tasks/work item(s). In a disposable repo copy, I inserted the sentence 'All implementation issues should enter the tracker ready to execute when possible.' ahead of the current helix-triage intro and ran HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_REPO_ROOT=<temp> HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_SKIP_REGRESSION=1 bash tests/validate-skills.sh; the validator still exited 0. That leaves the same semantic regression possible as long as the blanket priming adds a qualifier such as implementation between 'all' and 'issues'.
Suggested fix: Broaden the intro validation so it rejects blanket execution-ready priming even when adjectives or qualifiers appear between the quantifier and noun, or switch the regression fixture to compare against a more explicit allowlist/blocklist over the normalized intro text.
title: correctness: triage intro validator misses qualified blanket priming

Harmonize into: gofmt, go vet, golangci-lint config, staticcheck, error wrapping conventions, interface patterns, testing patterns (table-driven, testify if used), Go module conventions. Areas: all. Push concern into anneal, dun, grctool docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md.

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new story-level iterate artifact pattern is the shared directory `docs/helix/06-iterate/`. Under the same file, phase detection still uses `highest-artifact`, so once the iterate directory exists the rule can satisfy the iterate phase for every story regardless of whether that story has any iterate evidence. This collapses per-story state detection and can make partially completed stories appear to have reached ITERATE simply because the project has shared iterate docs.
Suggested fix: Remove the static iterate directory pattern from story-level state detection and make iterate determination query-only, or introduce a story-keyed evidence pattern that cannot match unrelated stories.

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:299
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new regression guard only checks for one exact sentence ("Every issue should enter the tracker ready to execute.") plus a few required substrings. In a disposable repo copy, I inserted the equivalent blanket priming sentence "Every issue should enter the tracker ready for execution when possible." ahead of the current intro in skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md, and bash tests/validate-skills.sh still exited 0. That means the validator does not actually satisfy helix-1356be0a's acceptance criterion that it fails when blanket execution-ready wording returns; semantically equivalent contradictory priming can ship undetected as long as the required new phrases remain somewhere in the file.
Suggested fix: Strengthen the validator so it checks the triage intro block semantically enough to reject equivalent blanket execution-ready claims, not only the exact old sentence. Keep the positive assertion that the intro scopes readiness to implementation beads and routes vague work to planning/polish or explicit not-execution-ready paths.

acceptance: The story-level iterate contract handles multiple phase:deploy issues deterministically. Either HELIX documents one canonical deploy bead per story, or the state rule/query explicitly requires all matching deploy issues closed and zero open deploy issues before a story enters ITERATE.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>workflows/state-rules.yml — iterate: type: iterate-evidence phase: iterate query_only: true query_command: "Inspect completed tracker issues labeled helix, phase:deploy, story:US-{id}. Treat closure of the story's deploy issue as the deterministic ITERATE threshold. Linked tracker follow-on work is supporting evidence when present; canonical iterate outputs remain shared iteration context and are not queried by story ID."</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-rules.yml:46
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new iterate query says to inspect completed tracker issues labeled `helix, phase:deploy, story:US-{id}` and then treats closure of the story's deploy issue as the deterministic ITERATE threshold. Elsewhere in the same contract, HELIX still allows deploy issue(s) per story (for example `transitions.story.build_to_deploy`, `transitions.story.deploy_to_iterate`, `workflows/state-machine.yaml`, and `workflows/artifact-hierarchy.md` all use plural `issue(s)`). Without an explicit rule that no open `phase:deploy` issues may remain, the story can be classified as ITERATE as soon as one deploy bead closes even if another deploy bead for the same story is still open.
Suggested fix: Define whether a story has exactly one canonical deploy bead or, if multiple deploy beads are allowed, update the iterate query/rule to require all matching `phase:deploy` issues to be closed and no open deploy issue to remain before the story is in ITERATE.
notes:

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts:1
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The FEAT-007 verification lane is green, but the Playwright suite still does not exercise the user-facing search workflow at all. `rg` over `website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts` shows page-load, navigation, and screenshot coverage only; there are no search input interactions or assertions on result routing. That violates the active `e2e-playwright` concern practice that every user-facing workflow have at least one end-to-end test, and it is why the broken `/glossary` search route in the rendered index was able to ship undetected in commit e61a4f6.
Suggested fix: Add a Playwright scenario that opens site search, queries at least glossary and CLI content, asserts the result URLs are valid, and verifies navigation succeeds when a result is selected.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.workflow — HELIX enforces Test-Driven Development (TDD) through a structured phase approach where tests are written BEFORE implementation. This ensures quality is built-in from the start and specifications are executable. Human creativity and AI capabilities collaborate throughout, with tests serving as the contract between design and implementation.</governing>
</context-digest>

The required validation suite `bash tests/helix-cli.sh` hangs late in the run while exercising `scripts/helix build --ddx-agent --dry-run`. The live temp-workspace process tree showed the harness blocked for at least 9 minutes in `ddx agent list` under the dry-run case, with no further log output.

Reproduction:
1. Run `bash tests/helix-cli.sh` in the HELIX repo.
2. Observe the suite pass most cases, then stall in a temp workspace on `bash /Users/erik/Projects/helix/scripts/helix build --ddx-agent --dry-run`.
3. Process tree shows a child `bash .../bin/ddx agent list` still running while the suite is otherwise quiescent.

This blocked full required validation for workflow-contract changes landed under `helix-9b6c9807`.

acceptance:
1. helix input "design a CRM" --autonomy low dispatches a prompt containing Autonomy level: low.
2. helix input --autonomy high "design a CRM" still dispatches Autonomy level: high.
3. Deterministic coverage in tests/helix-cli.sh exercises both flag positions and invalid autonomy.
4. bash tests/helix-cli.sh and git diff --check pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:3395
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The input subcommand parser only consumes --autonomy while it appears before the request. The published contract documents the request-first form (helix input "..." --autonomy high), but the current parser breaks on the first non-flag token and silently falls back to medium. Reproduction against the current branch shows bash scripts/helix input "design a CRM" --autonomy low dispatches a prompt containing Autonomy level: medium, which violates FEAT-011 / TD-011 semantics and can skip the confirmations required by low autonomy. The new deterministic coverage in tests/helix-cli.sh only exercises the unsupported flag-first form, so this documented CLI path remains broken and untested.
Suggested fix: Parse --autonomy in both positions accepted by the documented CLI surface, reject ambiguous argument layouts, and add deterministic tests that cover both helix input --autonomy high "..." and helix input "..." --autonomy low.
notes:

    <criterion name='flag-first autonomy syntax' status='pass' evidence='bash tests/helix-cli.sh covers helix input --autonomy high "design a CRM" -> Autonomy level: high'/>
    <criterion name='invalid and ambiguous input rejection' status='pass' evidence='bash tests/helix-cli.sh covers invalid autonomy, duplicate --autonomy, and ambiguous extra args'/>
    <criterion name='diff hygiene' status='pass' evidence='git diff --check'/>

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>none matched for tracker-state review</concerns>
<practices>Review tracker transitions against governing measure/report semantics before accepting state sync as correct · Favor durable evidence in bead metadata so later review/report passes do not require git archaeology</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity, so tracker state must preserve the true implementation context across later loop steps · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — tracker metadata mutation must surface durable state explicitly rather than leaving later actions to guess</adrs>
<governing>.ddx/beads.jsonl — helix-39fc1526 is closed after tracker-only commit 97f12a77f99a7fe447cbc25314e0b398e95e8159 but still lacks closing_commit_sha for implementation commit cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:77
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The automated review had to recover implementation commit cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c from git history because HEAD is the tracker-only close commit 97f12a77f99a7fe447cbc25314e0b398e95e8159 and the closed execution bead helix-39fc1526 still has no closing_commit_sha field in .ddx/beads.jsonl. The fresh-eyes-review action explicitly prefers closing_commit_sha in this situation so later review and report passes can resolve the true implementation commit without git archaeology. Leaving helix-39fc1526 closed without that pointer makes the tracker row incomplete and breaks the durable hand-off the automated close path is supposed to provide.
Suggested fix: Update the automated close bookkeeping so closing helix-39fc1526 persists closing_commit_sha=cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c, and ensure tracker-only close commits do not leave closed execution beads without their implementation-commit pointer.
title: correctness: persist closing_commit_sha for helix-39fc1526

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.prd — Alignment, review, measure, and report flows must capture every actionable</governing>
</context-digest>

Follow-on from alignment review issue hx-8e7f9b24.

Review finding from reconcile-alignment.
Scope: repo open queue
Category: regression
Severity: medium
Description: FEAT-006 requires triage and evolve to prepend a <context-digest> at bead creation, and EXECUTION says triage/evolve assemble a digest into every bead they create while polish refreshes stale digests. The earlier propagation fix closed under helix-7b3c6980, but the current live queue again contains recent open beads created after that closure with no digest at all, including helix-e393791b, helix-c3ff5fdf, helix-9648ca8d, helix-ce4b9180, helix-9e38e71e, helix-a938e147, and the CRM planning beads. Concern-resolution treats a missing digest as stale propagation evidence, so the queue has regressed from the FEAT-006 contract even after the earlier refresh pass.
Suggested fix: Restore digest assembly on the creation paths that are still bypassing it, backfill or supersede the recent open beads missing digests, and add deterministic coverage that fails when HELIX-created beads in the covered paths omit the digest without an explicit rationale.

acceptance: All child issues are closed. Every published skill directory basename matches its public skill name. Canonical project/user .agents/skills packaging is implemented and documented. description: Repackage HELIX as a portable Agent Skills library layered under the HELIX workflow and CLI without weakening the method. Preserve the core HELIX contract: command mirroring, authority-ordered planning, bounded actions, tracker-first execution, and workflow docs as the normative source of behavior. Scope includes directory/name alignment, canonical .agents/skills packaging, standards-oriented metadata and validation, and a docs rewrite that presents skills as the portable surface and the HELIX workflow/CLI as the stricter operating layer.
design: Deliver in four ordered slices. First, rename skill directories so published names, on-disk layout, and installer references match exactly. Second, adopt project-level and user-level .agents/skills packaging as the canonical layout, with .claude/skills retained only as a temporary compatibility mirror. Third, add the minimum metadata and deterministic validation needed to keep the package internally consistent. Fourth, rewrite the docs so the portable skill surface is clear while deeper HELIX semantics remain in the workflow contract.
title: Package HELIX as standards-compliant Agent Skills library

description:
Follow-on from alignment review issue hx-3f9f5857.

Review finding from reconcile-alignment.
Scope: repo demo and site surfaces
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The declared demo inventory no longer matches the shipped public surfaces. docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md still says the current demos are helix-quickstart, helix-concerns, and helix-evolve, while FEAT-007 requires Experiment as a first-class demo, website/content/docs/demos/_index.md embeds Experiment, and .github/workflows/pages.yml records quickstart, concerns, evolve, and experiment. The repo also ships docs/demos/helix-interactive/, but that demo has no corresponding concern override, FEAT-007 status, public embed, or Pages recording path, so its intended product status is unclear.
Suggested fix: Reconcile docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, FEAT-007/public demo documentation, and the Pages recording workflow so they agree on the authoritative demo set. Record an explicit disposition for helix-interactive: either make it a first-class public demo with site and CI coverage, or mark it as non-public/experimental and scope it out of the shipped demo inventory.

Scope includes write-path hardening, conflict detection, corruption handling, and deterministic race/corruption verification.

description: Fresh-eyes review of commit a06fd30 found two correctness issues in docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-03-27-repo.md. First, the queue-health summary records tracker state as '84 total / 8 open / 0 in-progress / 75 closed' without noting the remaining deferred issue, so the published totals are not self-consistent. Second, the report's Execution Order and closing recommendation say the strongest next move is hx-e7324e4f first, even though the same report states hx-c2dd330a is the only execution-safe ready issue. In an automated helix run context that distinction matters: strategic design priority and execution-safe next work are not the same thing. Update the durable report so its queue accounting is mechanically accurate and its next-step guidance distinguishes strategic ordering from the actual execution-safe queue.
design: Keep the alignment report durable and operationally safe. Either make the queue summary exhaustive, or explicitly state which statuses are omitted. Separate strategic follow-up ordering from execution-safe next work so the report does not imply that helix run should select a non-execution design issue ahead of the only execution-safe ready item.

The script now creates .agents and copies skills there, but it still redirects to .claude/settings.json without first creating .claude. On a clean workspace this fails immediately with: zsh: no such file or directory: .claude/settings.json. Evidence: docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh:144-146 and a clean tmpdir reproduction.
design: Keep the canonical .agents skill install path, but preserve the demo's Claude settings write by creating .claude before redirecting to .claude/settings.json, or by updating the demo flow so the settings file path is created safely.
title: Restore .claude directory creation in quickstart demo

acceptance: After a failed or timed-out implementation cycle, helix run does not retry on top of stale issue-scoped changes. The issue is returned to open before a fresh retry when cleanup succeeds, and the loop stops with a blocker when safe cleanup cannot be proven. Deterministic tests cover rc=1 and timeout cleanup paths, and bash tests/helix-cli.sh plus git diff --check pass.
description: After an implementation cycle fails with rc=1 or a watchdog timeout, the run loop currently retries against whatever partial state the failed attempt left behind. Production runs show this causes repeated timeouts and infinite retry loops because the issue stays claimed and the worktree remains dirty from the prior attempt.
design: At the failure boundary, inspect whether issue-scoped changes are left behind. Before retrying, either revert only the attributable partial changes and unclaim the issue, or stop with a blocker if cleanup cannot be done safely. Ensure timeout and non-timeout failures follow the same cleanup contract and add deterministic coverage for stale-claim release plus clean-worktree enforcement.
title: Clean worktree and release stale claims after failed implementation retries

description: Fresh-eyes review of commit 97e4817717c7bb64fef2358a0f77ce1e63a5762f found that repo alignment rerun follow-up hx-bf99e0ee was created from a Quality Findings entry with resolution direction 'quality-improvement', but it was filed as labels helix,phase:build,kind:review and execution-eligible=true. The alignment action requires quality-improvement findings to create backlog-type execution issues when warranted, and workflows/TRACKER.md maps improvement backlog items to helix,phase:iterate,kind:backlog. Reclassify this follow-up and any matching report text so the durable report and live queue no longer treat the tracker-note cleanup as build-phase execution work.
design: Preserve the underlying follow-up, but move it into the correct backlog classification. Update hx-bf99e0ee metadata and the durable alignment report so quality-only tracker hygiene work is represented as phase:iterate/kind:backlog rather than build/review, and ensure execution-safe guidance reflects that classification.
title: Reclassify alignment quality-improvement follow-up as backlog work

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/helix-cli.sh
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The deterministic test 'run should refuse stale close after supersession' fails with 'expected: open / actual: closed'. The failure is pre-existing (present at HEAD~1 as well as HEAD) and is not caused by commit 8c9fe95. The test expects an issue to remain open after supersession but the run loop closes it. Either the test expectation is stale or the supersession guard in the run loop has a bug.
Suggested fix: Investigate the test at the relevant section of tests/helix-cli.sh. Either fix the supersession guard logic in scripts/helix so it refuses to close superseded issues, or update the test expectation if the current behavior is intentional.
title: correctness: test 'run should refuse stale close after supersession' fails

acceptance: helix status reports persisted lifecycle state including active-run detection, current issue or focused epic, elapsed time, completion rate, recent results, blocker summary, and next recommended action from the run-controller state file. Deterministic coverage proves the status command reads the persisted state safely. bash tests/helix-cli.sh and git diff --check pass.
description: Add 'helix status' command that shows: is a run active (PID file check), current cycle/issue, elapsed time, completion rate, last N cycle results. Read from a state file written by the run loop.
design: Read persisted run-controller state written by helix run and render a structured lifecycle snapshot: active session, current issue or focused epic, elapsed time, completed/attempted counts, recent cycle results, blocker summary, and next recommended action. Fail closed when state is missing or stale rather than guessing.
title: Observability: helix status command for monitoring active runs

acceptance: tests/helix-cli.sh contains a deterministic case where the queue drains and the mocked check command exits non-zero before returning a NEXT_ACTION. In that scenario, helix run fails with the expected queue-drain check error message and does not trip a set -u unbound-variable error. bash tests/helix-cli.sh and git diff --check pass.
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit f43a70d6a8b275d5e041d459a891a3218f4a1770 found that the fix in scripts/helix for an unbound-variable crash after queue-drain check failure is not covered by the deterministic wrapper harness. tests/helix-cli.sh covers parsed NEXT_ACTION outcomes like WAIT and BACKFILL, but it does not simulate the failing path where run_check exits non-zero before the command-substitution assignment completes. Without a regression test, the original set -u failure can return unnoticed in future refactors of the run loop.
design: Add a deterministic wrapper test that forces the mock agent to fail on the queue-drain check path after ready work is exhausted. Assert that helix run --no-auto-review exits non-zero, emits 'helix: check failed after queue drain', and does not surface an unbound-variable error. Keep the test isolated to the queue-drain path so it proves the exact regression fixed by f43a70d6.
title: Add regression coverage for queue-drain check command-substitution failure

design: Two bash functions in tracker.sh. Return 0=ok, 1=hard error, 2=warning. Error messages to stderr with [triage] prefix.

The result is silent undercounting in per-cycle totals, blocker reports, and persisted run-controller state even when the watchdog and progress output otherwise work.
design: Update the Codex exec capture path so stdout and stderr are collected together before condense_codex_output and extract_codex_tokens run. Keep trailer parsing intact, preserve the existing condensed output behavior, and add deterministic coverage for a stderr-only token footer.
title: Capture Codex stderr token footer for run-loop accounting

description: Replace the current skip-after-2-attempts policy with exponential backoff. When an issue fails to complete, retry with increasing delay and effort before declaring it blocked. If the toolchain doesn't compile, try to fix it. Only give up after escalating effort (e.g. 1 attempt -> retry with more context -> retry with broader scope -> declare blocked). The agent should do hard things, not just pick low-hanging fruit.
title: Run loop: exponential backoff on hard issues instead of immediate bail

description: Fresh-eyes review of commit 2c17f5e737e11ecad1fa8b2c9f47f8da702f5a72 found that the implementation cycle appended another near-duplicate blocker note to issue hx-e7324e4f even though the decisive metadata and queue state were unchanged: the issue remained status=open with labels phase:design/kind:design, execution-eligible=false, and helix tracker ready --json --execution still returned only hx-c2dd330a. Repeated retries against the same non-execution design issue are bloating the canonical .helix/issues.jsonl record and making distinct state transitions harder to read.
design: Keep implementation revalidation evidence, but coalesce or suppress repeated notes when the blocker reason and relevant tracker state have not changed materially. Prefer one durable blocker note per distinct state transition rather than appending a new near-duplicate paragraph on every retry.

The underlying gap is a supplementary quality-improvement finding from AR-2026-03-27-repo.md rather than a story-build slice, so it should remain visible in the tracker without entering the immediate execution-safe queue for build work.
title: Deduplicate repeated implementation blocker notes for unchanged non-execution issues

acceptance: scripts/helix interprets machine-readable review status safely. helix run continues only after REVIEW_STATUS: CLEAN and stops or redirects on actionable findings. Deterministic tests cover both clean and finding-bearing review outputs.
description: Governing artifacts: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md. FEAT-002 and TD-002 require post-implementation review findings to be surfaced as actionable follow-up before the run loop advances, but scripts/helix currently treats review as a simple command success/failure and does not parse REVIEW_STATUS or issue counts. Add explicit review-result handling so non-clean review outcomes stop or redirect the loop deterministically.
design: Keep the current review hook, but parse the machine-readable review output and distinguish CLEAN from actionable findings. Add deterministic tests that simulate non-clean review output and verify the loop stops or creates follow-up work instead of silently continuing.
notes: Governing artifacts: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md.

acceptance: A non-clean post-implementation review in helix run creates or reopens tracker follow-up work before execution stops or redirects. Deterministic tests verify the tracker side effect and that the loop does not advance to the next issue without recorded follow-up work.
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit d4c2d4078006c9ab5bead1889831475aea725ffd found that scripts/helix now parses REVIEW_STATUS and stops on ISSUES_FOUND, but the TD-002 review-handling contract still requires review findings to create or reopen follow-up tracker work before the loop advances. Current behavior returns 0 from run_loop after logging the findings, with no tracker mutation or redirected follow-up flow. Add explicit follow-up creation or reopen behavior and cover it in the deterministic harness.
design: Preserve the new fail-closed parsing. When REVIEW_STATUS is non-clean and safely parseable, create a regression/follow-up issue or reopen the implementation issue according to the documented policy, then stop or redirect without advancing silently. Keep the behavior deterministic and machine-verifiable.
title: Create or reopen follow-up work when helix review finds issues

title: Harden tracker write paths and metadata mutation APIs

acceptance: When no parent/spec-id siblings exist, helix_select_batch returns ready non-epic siblings that share an area:* label with the primary issue. It still excludes skipped issues, epics, and unrelated work. Deterministic tests cover the fallback path and bash tests/helix-cli.sh plus git diff --check pass.
description: helix_select_batch currently batches only by shared parent or shared spec-id. Production queue analysis showed most leaf tasks lack parent metadata, which leaves the batching heuristic nearly useless even when related execution-safe issues clearly share the same area labels.
design: Keep parent and spec-id matching as the primary heuristics, but when they find no siblings, inspect ready execution-safe non-epic issues for shared area:* labels with the primary issue. Exclude skipped issues and unrelated phases, and leave the batch as a singleton when no area match exists.

acceptance: Line 622 of scripts/helix has a fallback so that ddx agent condense failure does not abort the script. Verified by: bash -c 'set -euo pipefail; echo test | false || echo fallback' prints fallback.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:622
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The script uses set -euo pipefail. At line 622, printf ... | ddx agent condense runs as a pipeline. If ddx agent condense exits non-zero (malformed input, bug, signal), pipefail propagates the failure and set -e aborts the script — the return "$_codex_rc" on line 623 never executes. The old condense_codex_output was a local shell function immune to this class of failure. Unguarded callers (frame, evolve, align, plan, experiment, recover) would crash if using the codex agent.
Suggested fix: Guard the pipe with a fallback: printf -- '%s\n' "$_codex_raw" | ddx agent condense || printf -- '%s\n' "$_codex_raw"
title: correctness: ddx agent condense failure crashes run loop via pipefail
